memoryArtifacts.headline: Whitney-on-Wye
memoryArtifacts.description:
Whitney-on-Wye
The name ‘de Whitney’ was assumed by Eustace, son of Turstin the Fleming, a companion of William the Conqueror, who was given possession of the land as mentioned in the Domesday Book. …

Whitney-on-Wye
The name ‘de Whitney’ was assumed by Eustace, son of Turstin the Fleming, a companion of William the Conqueror, who was given possession of the land as mentioned in the Domesday Book. …

zh.Whitney on Wye, Herefordshire, England: 英國英格蘭賀福爾特尼--也
zh.lifeSummary: 當Eustace de Whitney於1185年出生在英國賀福爾特尼--也時，他的父親Turstin de Flandrensis153歲，母親Agnes de Merleberge143歲。他和Ann Talbot至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1225年在他的家鄉享年40歲，埋葬在英國賀福爾特尼--也。

zh-hans.Whitney on Wye, Herefordshire, England: 英国英格兰賀福爾特尼--也
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eustace de Whitney于1185年出生在英国賀福爾特尼--也时，他的父亲Turstin de Flandrensis153岁，母亲Agnes de Merleberge143岁。他和Ann Talbot至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1225年在他的家乡享年40岁，埋葬在英国賀福爾特尼--也。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Whitney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Whitney in Herefordshire, the etymology of which is uncertain. The second element is Old English ēg ‘island, piece of higher ground in a low-lying area’; the first appears to be hwītan, which is either the genitive singular of an Old English byname Hwīta (meaning ‘white’), or the weak dative case (originally used after a preposition and article) of the adjective hwīt ‘white’. The name may also derive from Whitney (now Whitney Wood) in Stevenage (Hertfordshire), probably named from Old English hwītan ‘white’ + (ge)hæge ‘enclosure’.
History: John Whitney came from London, England, to Watertown, MA, in 1635, and had numerous prominent descendents.

variants.name: Witney
meanings: English: habitational name from Witney in Oxfordshire, which for centuries was a centre of the wool trade, famous in particular for the manufacture of blankets. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Witta (genitive Wittan) + Old English ēg, īeg ‘island’. There may have been some confusion with Whitney .

nameMeanings.name: Eustace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Old French form of the Late Greek names Eustakhios and Eustathios. These were evidently of separate origin, the former derived from eu ‘well, good’ + stakhys ‘grapes’, the latter from eu + stēnai ‘to stand’. However, the tradition is very confused. The name was introduced in this form to Britain by the Normans, among whom it was popular as a result of the fame of St Eustace , a Norman nobleman who was said to have been converted to Christianity by the vision of a crucifix between the antlers of the stag he was hunting.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Eustace de Whitney was born in 1185, in Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England, his father, Turstin de Flandrensis, was 153 and his mother, Agnes de Merleberge, was 143. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Ann Talbot. He died in 1225, in his hometown, at the age of 40, and was buried in Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eustace de Whitney est né en 1185, à Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Turstin de Flandrensis, avait 153 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Merleberge, avait 143 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Ann Talbot. Il est décédé en 1225, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eustace de Whitney 1185 in Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Turstin de Flandrensis 153 und seine Mutter Agnes de Merleberge 143. Er hatte mit Ann Talbot mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1225 im Alter von 40 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eustace de Whitney è nato il nel 1185 nel Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Turstin de Flandrensis, aveva 153 anni e il suo madre, Agnes de Merleberge, aveva 143 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Ann Talbot. Morì nel 1225, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolto a Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Eustace de Whitneyが1185年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィトニー-オン-ウイで生まれた時，父Turstin de Flandrensisは153歳，母Agnes de Merlebergeは143歳であった。彼はAnn Talbotとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1225年に故郷で40歳で死去し，イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィトニー-オン-ウイにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Eustace de Whitney(이)가 1185년 영국 허퍼드실르 휘트니--w예에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Turstin de Flandrensis(은)는 153세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Merleberge(은)는 143세였다. 그는 Ann Talbot(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1225년 그의 고향에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허퍼드실르 휘트니--w예에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eustace de Whitney nasceu em 1185, em Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Turstin de Flandrensis, tinha 153 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Merleberge, tinha 143 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Ann Talbot. Ele faleceu em 1225, em sua cidade natal, com 40 anos, e foi sepultado em Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eustace de Whitney родился в 1185 г. в местности уитни-он-ви, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Turstin de Flandrensis было 153 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Merleberge, 143. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Ann Talbot. Он умер в 1225 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности уитни-он-ви, хирефордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eustace de Whitney nació en 1185, en Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Turstin de Flandrensis, tenía 153 años y su mamá, Agnes de Merleberge, tenía 143 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija con Ann Talbot. Murió en 1225, en su pueblo, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultado en Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.
